story.iWant: a comprehensive end-to-end test validating the complete CQRS flow
story.soThat: I can prove the Walking Skeleton architecture works correctly

tasks.task: Implement test scenario: POST → Command → Event → Projection → GET
Validate Command dispatch, Event persistence, Projection update, Query retrieval
Commit: "Add Walking Skeleton end-to-end integration test"

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Test scenario: POST /widgets → CreateWidgetCommand → WidgetCreatedEvent → Projection updated → GET /widgets/:id returns data
Test validates: Command dispatch, Event persistence, Projection update, Query retrieval
Test measures and validates: API latency <200ms, Projection lag <10s
Test uses real PostgreSQL (Testcontainers), not mocks

doc.title: Decision Architecture
doc.snippet: Testcontainers for real dependencies. Full CQRS flow validation. Performance assertions. Eventually polling pattern for async projections. No mocks for stateful services.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: WalkingSkeletonIntegrationTest. Scenario: POST → Command → Event → Projection → GET. Validates all CQRS components. Measures performance. Real PostgreSQL. <2 minutes execution.

doc.title: Test Strategy
doc.snippet: Full Spring context. Testcontainers for infrastructure. @SpringBootTest with @Autowired field injection + init block. Eventually polling for async operations. Performance assertions.

dependency.name: testcontainers-postgresql
dependency.usage: Real PostgreSQL container for integration tests

constraints.constraint: Use @Autowired field injection + init block (NOT constructor injection)
Eventually polling pattern for projection updates

pattern.name: E2E Integration Test Pattern

ideas.idea: Test scenario: WidgetCreatedEvent persisted to domain_event_entry
Test scenario: GET /widgets/:id returns projected data
Test validates command dispatch via CommandGateway
Test validates event persistence in event store
Test validates projection update in widget_view
Test validates query retrieval via QueryGateway
Test does not use mocks for database operations
Test passes consistently (run 10 times, all pass)
Test execution time measured and <2 minutes
Manual: Document test as reference example in README
